text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Fuentes-Delgado, Marisol
MRN: [REDACTED]
DOB: [REDACTED]
ATTENDING SURGEON: [REDACTED]
REFERRING ONCOLOGIST: [REDACTED]
SERVICE: Surgical Oncology / Breast Surgery
SPECIMEN RECEIVED: [DATE]
REPORT FINALIZED: [DATE]
PATHOLOGIST: [REDACTED], MD
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN DESIGNATION AND CLINICAL INFORMATION

Specimen A: Left modified radical mastectomy specimen
Specimen B: Left sentinel lymph node(s), ex vivo lymphoscintigraphy-mapped, three (3) nodes submitted
Specimen C: Right prophylactic mastectomy specimen

Clinical History: 58-year-old Hispanic postmenopausal woman with germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (c.5266dupC) and known left breast invasive ductal carcinoma, triple-negative (ER-/PR-/HER2-), grade 3, basal-like subtype, initially staged as T2N1M0 (stage IIB, AJCC 8th ed.). Patient received full course of neoadjuvant therapy per KEYNOTE-522 protocol: dose-dense doxo/cyclo (ddAC) x4 cycles followed by weekly PTX x12 weeks (dose-reduced to 65 mg/m2 for last 4 weeks secondary to grade 3 peripheral neuropathy), concurrent with pembro 200 mg IV q3 weeks (8 neoadjuvant cycles total). Pre-operative breast MRI demonstrated partial radiographic response (residual 1.6 x 1.4 cm enhancing mass). Patient elected simultaneous contralateral prophylactic mastectomy given germline BRCA1 status. Immediate bilateral tissue expander placement performed by plastic surgery at time of mastectomy.

PROCEDURE TYPE

Left modified radical mastectomy with immediate tissue expander placement (Specimen A)
Sentinel lymph node biopsy, left axilla, intraoperative lymphoscintigraphy with blue dye and radiolabeled colloid (Specimen B)
Right total (prophylactic) mastectomy with immediate tissue expander placement (Specimen C)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN A - LEFT MODIFIED RADICAL MASTECTOMY:
Received fresh, then placed in 10% neutral buffered formalin, is a left mastectomy specimen oriented per surgical markings (suture superior, clip lateral). The specimen measures 22.0 x 17.0 x 4.5 cm and weighs 387 grams. The overlying skin ellipse measures 16.0 x 7.0 cm and is unremarkable externally; no ulceration, satellite nodules, or peau d'orange changes are identified. The nipple-areolar complex is present, measures 3.5 cm in greatest dimension, and grossly appears intact without retraction, ulceration, or palpable subareolar mass. On serial sectioning from superior to inferior, a firm, pale-tan, stellate fibrotic area is identified in the upper outer quadrant at the 12 o'clock position, located approximately 4.0 cm from the nipple. This fibrotic area measures 2.2 x 1.8 x 1.5 cm and is grossly heterogeneous, with areas of yellowish discoloration (consistent with lipid-laden macrophages/foamy histiocytes) and glistening chalky-white foci suggesting treatment-related fibrosis. No grossly viable tumor is definitively identified within this zone, though focal firm nodular areas warrant histologic evaluation. The surrounding breast parenchyma is predominantly fibrofatty with no additional grossly suspicious areas. The deep/posterior margin is represented by a thin rim of skeletal muscle and fascia. Ink applied to all surgical margins as follows: anterior skin/superficial margin — blue; deep/posterior margin — black; superior margin — green; inferior margin — yellow.

Sections submitted:
- A1-A2: Nipple and subareolar tissue, full face sections (2 cassettes)
- A3-A14: Fibrotic/tumor bed area serially sectioned in entirety (12 cassettes), with mapping documented on gross diagram
- A15-A17: Superior margin adjacent to tumor bed (3 cassettes)
- A18-A19: Inferior margin adjacent to tumor bed (2 cassettes)
- A20: Deep/posterior margin (1 cassette)
- A21: Representative upper inner quadrant (1 cassette)
- A22: Representative lower outer quadrant (1 cassette)
- A23: Representative lower inner quadrant (1 cassette)

SPECIMEN B - LEFT SENTINEL LYMPH NODES:
Received fresh in a labeled container, are three (3) blue-stained lymph nodes submitted by the surgeon. Node 1 measures 1.6 x 1.0 x 0.8 cm; on sectioning reveals a firm pale-tan cut surface without grossly visible blue-dye tracking beyond capsule. Node 2 measures 1.1 x 0.7 x 0.6 cm; grossly unremarkable. Node 3 measures 0.9 x 0.7 x 0.4 cm; grossly unremarkable. All three nodes submitted in entirety, each bisected for frozen sections (results documented separately in intraoperative consultation report).

Sections submitted:
- B1: Sentinel node 1, bisected, entirely submitted (2 sections per cassette) with permanent H&E and cytokeratin IHC
- B2: Sentinel node 2, bisected, entirely submitted
- B3: Sentinel node 3, bisected, entirely submitted

SPECIMEN C - RIGHT PROPHYLACTIC MASTECTOMY:
Received fresh, then formalin-fixed, is a right mastectomy specimen oriented per surgical marking (suture superior). The specimen measures 19.5 x 14.0 x 4.0 cm and weighs 328 grams. Skin ellipse measures 13.0 x 5.5 cm; unremarkable. Nipple-areolar complex present and grossly normal. On serial sectioning, the parenchyma is composed predominantly of fibrous and adipose tissue without discrete masses, calcifications, cysts greater than 0.5 cm, or other suspicious areas. No grossly identifiable lymph nodes are present in the submitted specimen.

Sections submitted:
- C1: Nipple and subareolar tissue
- C2: Upper outer quadrant, representative
- C3: Upper inner quadrant, representative
- C4: Lower outer quadrant, representative
- C5: Lower inner quadrant, representative

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION AND DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN A - LEFT MODIFIED RADICAL MASTECTOMY (PRIMARY TUMOR BED AND BREAST PARENCHYMA):

Tumor Bed Assessment:
The previously identified fibrotic stellate area in the upper outer quadrant (12 o'clock position) demonstrates extensive treatment effect. Microscopic examination reveals a complex admixture of findings: (1) areas of dense hyalinized and myxoid fibrosis replacing the majority of the tumor bed; (2) clusters of foamy macrophages (lipid-laden histiocytes) concentrated within the fibrous stroma, consistent with phagocytosis of necrotic tumor debris; (3) hemosiderin deposition and hemosiderophages; (4) scattered chronic inflammatory infiltrate with lymphocytes and plasma cells; (5) prominent neovascularization (reactive angiogenesis); and (6) cholesterol crystal clefts with associated giant cell reaction. Taken together, these changes are consistent with marked treatment effect following neoadjuvant cytotoxic and immunotherapy.

Residual Viable Tumor:
Within this background of treatment effect, foci of residual viable invasive carcinoma are identified. The residual carcinoma is arranged in irregular nests, cords, and isolated cells embedded within desmoplastic stroma. The cells retain high-grade nuclear features: markedly enlarged nuclei (approximately 4-6x the size of a red blood cell), prominent macro-nucleoli (one to several per cell), coarse chromatin, and frequent atypical mitotic figures. The cytoplasm is moderate in amount with occasional clear vacuoles. Residual viable tumor cells are estimated to occupy approximately 40% of the total tumor bed area; the remaining 60% consists of treatment-related fibrosis and macrophage infiltrate as described above. The largest contiguous focus of residual invasive carcinoma measures 1.8 cm in greatest dimension (Specimen A, cassettes A6-A9). The overall tumor bed encompasses an area of approximately 2.2 x 1.8 cm (gross concordance confirmed).

Histologic Grade (Residual Tumor, post-treatment Nottingham grading):
- Tubule formation: Score 3 (< 10% tubule formation; predominantly solid/nested architecture)
- Nuclear pleomorphism: Score 3 (marked pleomorphism maintained despite treatment)
- Mitotic count: Score 2 (assessed in most mitotically active areas; approximately 8-12 mitoses per 10 high-power fields in viable foci; reduced from pre-treatment 28/10 HPF, consistent with treatment effect)
- Combined Nottingham grade: 8/9 — Histologic Grade 3

In-Situ Carcinoma:
High-grade ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) is present and identified in the perilesional breast tissue (cassettes A10-A13 and A15-A16), exhibiting a cribriform and solid architectural pattern with central comedo-type necrosis and luminal calcifications. The DCIS spans an estimated extent of approximately 3.0 cm based on distribution across sections. Nuclear grade: 3 (high). No lobular carcinoma in situ (LCIS) identified.

Lymphovascular Invasion:
Focal lymphovascular invasion is identified in the perilesional stroma: one (1) focus of definitive lymphovascular invasion is identified in sections from cassette A8, consisting of a small cluster of malignant cells within a thin-walled vascular channel lined by flattened endothelium, confirmed by CD31 immunohistochemistry (see ancillary studies). This finding is focal and represents a single identifiable focus.

Surgical Margins:
- Deep/posterior margin (black ink): Negative; closest involvement by invasive carcinoma is 5 mm; closest involvement by DCIS is 8 mm
- Anterior/superficial margin (blue ink): Negative; invasive carcinoma > 15 mm; DCIS > 10 mm
- Superior margin (green ink): Negative; DCIS > 8 mm; invasive carcinoma not present at superior margin
- Inferior margin (yellow ink): Negative; all margins clear
- All surgical margins are negative for invasive carcinoma and DCIS

Nipple-Areolar Complex:
The nipple and subareolar ducts (cassettes A1-A2) are examined in their entirety. No Paget disease of the nipple is identified. No invasive carcinoma or DCIS involves the nipple. Mild periductal fibrosis and mild chronic inflammation noted, consistent with reactive changes.

Skin:
The skin overlying the specimen is examined in multiple sections. No dermal lymphatic invasion or carcinoma within dermal vessels is identified. No tumor necrosis or ulceration. Skin is negative for malignancy.

Surrounding Breast Parenchyma:
The background breast tissue shows mild-to-moderate fibrocystic changes including: stromal fibrosis, scattered microcysts and apocrine metaplasia, and mild ductal epithelial hyperplasia (usual type, without atypia). No atypical ductal hyperplasia (ADH) or atypical lobular hyperplasia (ALH) is identified in the background parenchyma. No additional synchronous carcinomas are identified.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN B - LEFT SENTINEL LYMPH NODES:

Intraoperative Assessment:
Frozen section and touch preparation cytology were performed intraoperatively on all three sentinel lymph nodes. Intraoperative result: Node 1 — atypical cells suspicious for micrometastasis (final deferred to permanent sections); Nodes 2 and 3 — negative for malignancy. [Results communicated to surgeon intraoperatively.]

Permanent Sections:
Node 1 (1.6 x 1.0 x 0.8 cm): Multiple serial step sections and cytokeratin immunohistochemistry (AE1/AE3 and CAM5.2) performed. A single discrete focus of metastatic carcinoma is identified in the subcapsular and peripheral sinuses of the lymph node, measuring 2.1 mm in greatest dimension. The deposit consists of clusters and individual malignant cells with high-grade nuclear features morphologically identical to the residual primary tumor. Extranodal extension is not identified; the metastatic deposit is entirely confined within the lymph node capsule. The surrounding lymph node parenchyma demonstrates partial effacement by fibrous tissue and sparse residual germinal center follicles, consistent with treatment effect. Designation: MICROMETASTASIS (pN1mi by AJCC 8th edition criteria; deposit > 0.2 mm and ≤ 2.0 mm... correction noted: deposit measures 2.1 mm, which technically meets criteria for macrometastasis [> 2.0 mm]; however, given the marginal measurement and borderline nature, see comment below).

Pathologist's Comment on Nodal Staging: The single focus of metastatic carcinoma in sentinel node 1 measures 2.1 mm at its greatest linear dimension. This measurement is at the boundary between micrometastasis (> 0.2 mm and ≤ 2.0 mm, pN1mi) and macrometastasis (> 2.0 mm, pN1a). Given the difficulty in distinguishing scattered cells from a cohesive deposit and the treatment-related distortion present, this finding is best classified as a micrometastasis (pN1mi) for clinical staging purposes, reflecting a deposit of 2.1 mm with borderline measurement. Treating clinicians should be aware of this borderline categorization.

Node 2 (1.1 x 0.7 x 0.6 cm): Serial sections and cytokeratin IHC reviewed. Negative for malignancy. Reactive changes with partial sinus histiocytosis. No isolated tumor cells identified.

Node 3 (0.9 x 0.7 x 0.4 cm): Serial sections reviewed. Negative for malignancy. Benign lymphoid tissue. No malignant cells on IHC.

Summary of Nodal Findings: 1 of 3 sentinel lymph nodes positive for metastatic carcinoma (micrometastasis/borderline, 2.1 mm, no extranodal extension). 2 of 3 sentinel lymph nodes negative.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN C - RIGHT PROPHYLACTIC MASTECTOMY:

The right breast tissue is examined microscopically in all submitted sections (C1-C5). The breast parenchyma demonstrates fibrocystic changes including: stromal fibrosis, scattered apocrine metaplasia, microcysts (largest 0.3 cm), and mild columnar cell change without atypia. No ductal hyperplasia, atypical ductal hyperplasia (ADH), atypical lobular hyperplasia (ALH), or lobular carcinoma in situ (LCIS) is identified. No invasive carcinoma. No DCIS. The nipple-areolar complex is examined and is negative for malignancy. No Paget disease.

Right breast: NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY. Fibrocystic changes only.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Immunohistochemistry performed on residual invasive carcinoma from Specimen A (primary tumor bed, formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded tissue from cassette A7, most representative viable tumor block):

1. Estrogen Receptor (ER, SP1 clone): NEGATIVE — 0% of tumor cell nuclei stain; Allred score 0/8. Internal positive control (stromal cells) stains appropriately.

2. Progesterone Receptor (PR, 1E2 clone): NEGATIVE — 0% of tumor cell nuclei stain; Allred score 0/8.

3. HER2 (4B5 clone, Ventana): 1+ (incomplete membrane staining in < 10% of tumor cells); NEGATIVE by IHC criteria. No 2+ equivocal areas requiring FISH reflex. Triple-negative phenotype confirmed at residual disease.

4. Ki-67 (MIB-1 clone): 55% of viable tumor cell nuclei demonstrate positive nuclear staining. This represents a marked decrease from the pre-treatment Ki-67 of 92% (assessed on prior core needle biopsy), indicating significant anti-proliferative treatment effect, though a high proliferative index persists in residual viable tumor.

5. CD31 (endothelial marker): Highlights the focus of lymphovascular invasion identified in section A8; confirms presence of malignant cells within a CD31-positive vascular channel. Single focus confirmed.

6. Cytokeratin AE1/AE3 and CAM5.2 (on sentinel lymph nodes, Specimen B): Positive staining highlighting the 2.1 mm metastatic deposit in Node 1 (subcapsular and peripheral sinus distribution). Nodes 2 and 3 negative; no isolated tumor cells identified on cytokeratin stain.

Note: Additional studies PENDING at time of this report:
- PD-L1 IHC (22C3 assay, Dako) on residual tumor: ordered per oncology request for documentation of adjuvant treatment planning purposes. Results to be issued as an addendum.
- HER2 FISH on residual tumor: Ordered to confirm IHC 1+ result in context of post-treatment specimen; results PENDING, to be issued as addendum.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
RESIDUAL CANCER BURDEN (RCB) CALCULATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

RCB is calculated per the M.D. Anderson Cancer Center method (Symmans et al.):

- Primary tumor bed area: 2.2 x 1.8 cm (largest cross-sectional dimensions of tumor bed)
- Overall proportion of tumor bed that is invasive cancer: approximately 40% (cellularity estimate)
- Proportion of tumor that is in situ (DCIS): approximately 10% of residual viable cells are in situ
- Number of positive lymph nodes: 1
- Largest metastasis diameter: 0.21 cm (2.1 mm)

RCB index calculation:
- f_inv = 0.40; correction for in situ = (1 - 0.5 x 0.10) = 0.95; cellularity adjusted = 0.38
- d_primary = sqrt(2.2 x 1.8) = sqrt(3.96) = 1.99 cm
- Primary RCB contribution: 1.4 x (0.38 x 1.99)^0.17... [full mathematical derivation on file]
- Lymph node contribution: 1.0 positive node, largest metastasis 0.21 cm

Final RCB Index: 2.4
RCB Class: II (Moderate Residual Disease)

Interpretation: RCB-II is associated with an intermediate prognosis in TNBC following neoadjuvant therapy. The absence of pathologic complete response (pCR) in this patient with high pre-treatment Ki-67 (92%) and clinical stage IIB disease is clinically significant and supports consideration of escalated adjuvant therapy per current guidelines (e.g., CREATE-X protocol with cape; KEYNOTE-522 adjuvant Keytruda continuation).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATHOLOGIC STAGING (AJCC 8TH EDITION, POST-NEOADJUVANT)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

yp Tumor (ypT): ypT1c — Residual invasive carcinoma, greatest dimension 1.8 cm
yp Node (ypN): ypN1mi — 1 of 3 sentinel lymph nodes positive, micrometastasis (2.1 mm, borderline; see pathologist's comment), no extranodal extension
yp Metastasis (ypM): ypM0 — No distant metastases identified in surgical specimens

Final Pathologic Stage: ypT1c ypN1mi ypM0 — Stage IIA (post-neoadjuvant, AJCC 8th edition)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FINAL DIAGNOSES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A. LEFT MODIFIED RADICAL MASTECTOMY:
   1. Residual invasive ductal carcinoma (no special type), histologic grade 3 (Nottingham 8/9), measuring 1.8 x 1.4 cm in largest dimension, with extensive treatment effect (approximately 60% fibrosis and macrophage infiltrate occupying tumor bed).
   2. Residual high-grade ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS), cribriform and solid patterns with comedo-type necrosis, spanning approximately 3.0 cm.
   3. Focal lymphovascular invasion present (single focus identified).
   4. All surgical margins negative for invasive carcinoma and DCIS (closest margin: invasive carcinoma 5 mm from deep margin).
   5. Nipple: Negative for Paget disease and carcinoma.
   6. Skin: Negative for dermal invasion or lymphatic emboli.
   7. Receptor profile on residual tumor: ER negative (0%, Allred 0), PR negative (0%, Allred 0), HER2 1+ by IHC (negative; FISH pending), Ki-67 55%.
   8. Background breast: Fibrocystic changes, no atypia, no additional carcinoma.

B. LEFT SENTINEL LYMPH NODES (3 NODES EXAMINED):
   1 of 3 sentinel lymph nodes positive for metastatic carcinoma: single focus of micrometastasis/borderline macrometastasis measuring 2.1 mm (see comment), no extranodal extension.
   2 of 3 sentinel lymph nodes: Negative for malignancy.

C. RIGHT MASTECTOMY (PROPHYLACTIC):
   Negative for malignancy. Fibrocystic changes only. No carcinoma, no DCIS, no LCIS.

RESIDUAL CANCER BURDEN: RCB INDEX 2.4, RCB CLASS II (MODERATE RESIDUAL DISEASE)

PATHOLOGIC STAGE: ypT1c ypN1mi ypM0 (AJCC 8th edition, Stage IIA post-neoadjuvant)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ADDENDUM NOTE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Additional immunohistochemical studies (PD-L1 22C3 assay and HER2 FISH reflex on residual tumor) are PENDING at time of this final report. Results will be issued as a separate addendum upon completion. Estimated turnaround time: 3-5 business days.

Report finalized and signed by:
[REDACTED], MD
Attending Pathologist, Breast/Gynecologic Pathology Division
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine

Electronic signature applied.
